articles.title: Remembering a Brave TV Star
articles.summary: Mel Schilling was a star on a TV show called Married at First Sight in Australia and the UK. She helped people find love as a relationship coach. Sadly, she died after being sick with cancer for two years. Her friend Paul, who worked with her on the show, shared a nice message about her. He said Mel was fearless, tough, and very strong. Even when she was traveling and getting treatment, she always smiled and never complained. Mel cared a lot about women and the LGBTQ+ community. She also loved her family, like her daughter and husband. The TV channel said they will keep showing the show because Mel wanted to help others find happiness. Everyone will miss her very much.

articles.title: Coco Gauff Fights Doubts to Reach Tennis Semi-Final
articles.summary: Coco Gauff is a young tennis star who just reached the semi-finals of the Miami Open for the first time. She beat Belinda Bencic in a tough match. Even though she is a champion, Gauff sometimes feels like she doesn't deserve her success, which is called imposter syndrome. She talks about learning to believe in herself with help from her coach. Gauff is now ranked fourth in the world and could move up if she wins more matches. Next, she will play Karolina Muchova in the semi-finals. The article also mentions other players like Elena Rybakina and Jessica Pegula in the tournament.

articles.title: OpenAI Stops Sora App and Disney Deal
articles.summary: OpenAI has closed its Sora app, which made videos using AI from simple words. It also ended a partnership with Disney, a big entertainment company. OpenAI says it wants to focus on other projects like robots that can help people with real tasks. Disney respects this decision and will work with other AI companies to use technology safely without breaking rules. Sora started in 2024 and made very realistic videos, but some people worried about copyright issues. Disney had a deal with OpenAI to let users make videos with characters like Mickey Mouse. Now, OpenAI will not make video tools anymore but will keep working on other AI technologies.

articles.title: A Tiny Brain Implant That Sends Signals Without Wires
articles.summary: Scientists at Cornell University made a very small brain implant that is tinier than a grain of salt. This tiny device can sit on a grain of salt and send brain activity information from a living animal without using wires. It works for more than a year. The implant uses light to send the brain signals. It has a special part called a semiconductor diode that catches light to power it and sends light to share data. The device is about 300 microns long and 70 microns wide, which is super tiny. This could help doctors monitor brains better in the future, like during MRI scans, and might be used in other body parts too. It is called a microscale optoelectronic tetherless electrode, or MOTE for short.

articles.title: Manchester's Music Magic: A Fun Story for Kids
articles.summary: Manchester is a city in the UK that loves music! A long time ago, in the late 1980s and early 1990s, it was famous for cool bands and parties, called the Madchester era. People like DJ Steve Lamacq remember how exciting it was. Now, 35 years later, Manchester is becoming a music hotspot again. Big events like the Brit Awards and Mobo Awards are happening there, and a new arena called Co-op Live is opening. The mayor, Andy Burnham, says more young musicians are moving to Manchester, and the city is full of energy. This shows Manchester is giving UK music a new life!

articles.title: Podcast Hosts Say Goodbye After 10 Years
articles.summary: Audrey and Tolly, the hosts of The Receipts Podcast, are stepping down after 10 years. They said this was a very hard decision that made them cry a lot. The podcast started in 2016 to let black women talk about many topics, and they made almost 300 episodes. They also had a popular show on the BBC and a deal with Spotify. Even though they were successful, they feel it is time to stop hosting to try new things. The podcast will not end; new hosts will take over while Audrey and Tolly help from behind the scenes. They plan to find new hosts through a search like a talent show, looking for fresh voices and ideas, and they want to help people learn even if they do not get picked.
